semantic.role: Defines the purpose, scope, and responsibilities of the ArchSpine mirror system—a centralized governance layer that captures, maintains, and evolves architectural documentation and rules for a software project.
responsibilities: Documenting the architectural role and boundaries of the ArchSpine system
Listing specific responsibilities such as rule enforcement, view generation, state synchronization, and documentation maintenance
Defining the maintenance scope: which artifacts (schemas, rules, views, indexes) are managed and how they are kept in sync with the codebase
Establishing the contractual guarantees between the mirror system and the actual project
outOfScope: Implementation details of the source code under governance
External integrations beyond the defined MCP and CLI interfaces

English.purpose: This document explains why the ArchSpine mirror system exists: to provide a trusted, always-up-to-date documentation scaffold that mirrors the real project's architecture, rules, and health indicators. It ensures that both human developers and AI agents have a consistent, authoritative source of truth about the project's design and constraints.
English.context_and_audience: Primary audience: software architects, technical leads, and documentation maintainers who need to understand how ArchSpine fits into their development workflow. Secondary audience: AI agents and automation tools that consume the structured documentation (JSON indexes, rule files, view data) to make informed decisions about code changes and governance.
key_takeaways: ArchSpine is a 'documentation mirror'—its content is a derivable projection of the actual project, kept in sync via scans and rule checks.
It enforces architectural rules (layering, naming conventions) and surfaces violations that must be addressed.
It generates multiple views (architecture diagrams, risk hotspots, agent briefings) from a unified index.
Maintenance is automated: tasks like reconciliation, post-commit derivation, and view generation run as part of the pipeline.
The system is designed to be language-agnostic, multilingual (atlas/English, atlas/Simplified Chinese), and extensible through schemas and rule definitions.
